  • Project location: England only
  • Grant amount: Between 100,000 and 225,000 pounds
  • Decision timeline: Applicants can expect a decision within approximately 21 weeks of applying
  • Programme status: Open to applications

Prospective applicants are strongly encouraged to read all available guidance on the National Lottery Community Fund website before beginning their application, and to make use of any available support resources to ensure their application is as strong as possible.

For organizations that have previously been unsuccessful in applying for larger funding, or that feel uncertain about whether they qualify, the fund’s co-design with The Phoenix Community Trust and their partners means there is genuine community expertise embedded in the programme to support the process.

APPLY HERE

After You Apply

Once an application has been submitted, The National Lottery Community Fund will review it for eligibility and quality. Organizations can expect to receive a decision within approximately 21 weeks. Successful organizations will receive multi-year funding and the support needed to begin implementing their plans.

The fund is also designed to generate learning — meaning that The National Lottery Community Fund will be actively working with funded organizations to understand what is working, what challenges arise, and how the experience of this fund can improve future grant-making practice and influence the wider sector.

Visit HERE for more information about the Step Forward Fund 2026.

Conclusion

The Step Forward Fund represents a genuine and substantive commitment to equity in community funding in England. For grassroots organizations led by and serving Global Majority and racially minoritised communities that have been navigating the challenging gap between small project grants and the larger investment they need to grow, this fund could be genuinely transformational. With between 100,000 and 225,000 pounds available per organization, a multi-year funding model that allows for real organizational development, and a design process that has centered the voices and language of the communities it serves, The Step Forward Fund is a rare opportunity to take a meaningful step forward.

If your organization has received a small National Lottery grant, has tried and not yet succeeded with a Reaching Communities application, is led by and serves Global Majority and racially minoritised communities, and has an annual income below 300,000 pounds, then this fund was designed with you in mind. The programme is currently open to applications. Review the full eligibility criteria on The National Lottery Community Fund website, prepare a thoughtful and detailed application, and take the step forward your organization has been working toward.

Visit OFY to discover more global Grant opportunities.

The post Step Forward Fund 2026: National Lottery Grants for Grassroots Organizations in England appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant 2026: Everything Nonprofit Organizations in New York, New Jersey, and Utah Need to Know
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Goldman Sachs, one of the world’s most recognized financial institutions, is once again opening its Community Development Grants program for 2026 — and nonprofit organizations working to advance economic opportunity in low- and moderate-income communities across New York, New Jersey, and Utah should take note. Through two distinct funding initiatives — the Change Makers Initiative […]

The post Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant 2026: Everything Nonprofit Organizations in New York, New Jersey, and Utah Need to Know appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Goldman Sachs, one of the world’s most recognized financial institutions, is once again opening its Community Development Grants program for 2026 — and nonprofit organizations working to advance economic opportunity in low- and moderate-income communities across New York, New Jersey, and Utah should take note. Through two distinct funding initiatives — the Change Makers Initiative and the Equity Builders Initiative — Goldman Sachs is providing both one-time and multi-year general operating grants to high-impact nonprofits committed to transforming the lives of individuals and families in underserved communities.

This article provides a thorough and detailed breakdown of the Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant program for 2026, covering what it is, the two initiatives on offer, who is eligible, what the assessment criteria are, how the funding works, the timeline, and how to apply.

What Is the Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Program?

The Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions program is a philanthropic grant initiative rooted in the firm’s long-standing belief that strong communities are the foundation of economic growth and a prosperous society. Through its Community Development Grants program, Goldman Sachs provides funding to nonprofit organizations that are committed to advancing economic opportunity and mobility in low- and moderate-income (LMI) communities across the United States.

The programme has already demonstrated meaningful impact. According to self-reported data from 2025 grantees collected in January 2026, the program has:

  • Reached over 808,000 community members
  • Served more than 2,000 small businesses
  • Supported 30 grant champions across its initiatives

These figures reflect the scale and depth of Goldman Sachs’s investment in community development — and the 2026 cycle represents the next chapter in that commitment.

The Four Pillars of Goldman Sachs Community Development

The Goldman Sachs Community Development Grants program is structured around four core areas of focus, all of which are designed to address the most pressing and interconnected challenges facing low- and moderate-income communities:

1. Affordable Housing Solutions

This pillar focuses on providing access to affordable, safe, and stable housing for LMI individuals and families. Housing instability is one of the most significant barriers to economic mobility, and Goldman Sachs recognizes that sustainable community development must begin with ensuring that people have a secure place to live.

2. Essential Community Services

This pillar encompasses a broad range of critical support systems, including social services, education, job training, and broader community development initiatives. The goal is to ensure that LMI individuals have access to the services and skills they need to participate fully in the economy and society.

3. Small Business Growth

This pillar focuses on providing catalytic capital and philanthropic investment in economic development, with a particular emphasis on under-resourced small businesses. Small businesses are the backbone of local economies, and targeted support for entrepreneurs in LMI communities can generate jobs, build wealth, and revitalize neighborhoods.

4. Neighborhood Revitalization

This pillar supports activities that help attract new businesses and residents to underserved neighborhoods or retain existing ones. Neighborhood revitalization is about creating conditions for long-term, sustainable community prosperity — not just addressing immediate needs.

The Two Grant Initiatives: Change Makers and Equity Builders

The Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions program for 2026 operates through two distinct funding initiatives, each with a different structure and purpose. Both are currently open for applications.

Initiative 1: The Change Makers Initiative

The Change Makers Initiative is an annual funding opportunity that provides a one-time general operating grant to high-impact nonprofit organizations located in New York, New Jersey, and Utah. This initiative is designed for nonprofits that are driving innovation and measurable impact in LMI communities through differentiated approaches and community-driven solutions.

Key themes that have defined recent Change Makers cohorts include:

  • Access to Housing: Expediting access to affordable apartments for homeless New Yorkers with housing vouchers
  • Embracing AI: Leading artificial intelligence adoption initiatives and ensuring LMI communities are not left behind in the digital economy, through literacy training and organizational transformation
  • Empowering Parents: Providing free, high-quality childcare that enables parents to work toward financial autonomy and security
  • Building Financial Futures: Offering financial coaching and debt relief services that open pathways to homeownership and long-term economic stability

Past Change Makers grantees include a wide range of recognized organizations such as Brooklyn Public Library (LevelUp), DataKind, iMentor NYC, Rising Tide Capital, Neighborhood Trust Financial Partners, The HOPE Program, BRICK Networks, DreamYard Project, Van Alen Institute, and many others across New York, New Jersey, and Utah.

Initiative 2: The Equity Builders Initiative

The Equity Builders Initiative provides multi-year, general operating grants to nonprofit organizations working to build organizational capacity and sustainably deepen their impact in the LMI communities they serve. Unlike the Change Makers one-time grant, Equity Builders is designed for organizations that are ready to take a longer-term view of their growth and impact — and that need sustained, flexible investment to do so.

Multi-year general operating grants are among the most valuable forms of philanthropic support available to nonprofits, because they provide the financial stability and flexibility needed to invest in staff, systems, leadership, and long-term strategic planning — not just individual programmes or projects.

Both initiatives are currently open for applications. Eligible organizations should carefully review both options and determine which initiative best aligns with their organizational stage and funding needs.

Who Is Eligible to Apply?

To be considered for either the Change Makers or Equity Builders initiative, applicants must meet all of the following eligibility criteria:

  1. Be a domestic, nonprofit, tax-exempt organization registered under Section 501(c)(3) of the United States Internal Revenue Code
  2. Have a clear and demonstrable focus on community development, specifically addressing the needs of LMI areas or households
  3. Operate primarily in one of the following specified geographic regions:
    • New York: New York County, Bronx County, Kings County, Richmond County, or Queens County
    • New Jersey: Hudson County or Essex County
    • Utah: Salt Lake County, Summit County, or Tooele County

These geographic parameters are important. The grant is not available to nonprofits operating outside of these specific counties, even if they are based in the broader states of New York, New Jersey, or Utah. Organizations must confirm that their primary area of operation falls within the designated counties before applying.

Assessment Criteria: What Goldman Sachs Is Looking For

Applications to the Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant will be evaluated based on — but not limited to — the following three core criteria:

1. Impact

  • Applications should specify measurable outcomes in LMI communities
  • Applicants must demonstrate a documented history of effectively meeting community needs
  • Goldman Sachs is looking for evidence that the organization’s work produces real, quantifiable change in the lives of LMI individuals and families

2. Community Engagement

  • Applications should demonstrate meaningful engagement with LMI communities throughout the design and implementation of programming
  • Goldman Sachs prioritizes community-driven solutions — meaning that the people most affected by the challenges being addressed should be active participants in shaping the solutions
  • Organizations that position community members as partners rather than passive recipients of services will be viewed favorably

3. Innovation

  • Applications should demonstrate a differentiated approach to community development
  • Applicants should articulate clearly how their model advances beyond existing solutions and delivers measurable impact for LMI communities
  • Goldman Sachs is particularly interested in organizations that are finding new and more effective ways to address longstanding challenges in housing, employment, financial inclusion, education, and small business development

Application Timeline

The Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant operates on the following timeline for the 2026 cycle:

  • Phase 1 — Application Submission: All eligible applicants must submit their completed application by July 24, 2026, at 11:59 PM Eastern Standard Time (EST). This is a firm deadline and late submissions will not be considered.
  • Phase 2 — Award Decisions: Award decisions will be finalized in the fourth quarter of 2026. Successful awardees will be notified ahead of a public announcement.
  • Phase 3 — Funding Disbursement: Funding will be disbursed to awarded nonprofits by the end of 2026, for implementation and programming throughout 2027.

How to Apply

Organizations wishing to apply to either the Change Makers or Equity Builders initiative should:

  1. Review the 2026 Application Questions PDF, available on the Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions webpage
  2. Consult the Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) document for additional guidance and clarification
  3. Submit applications through the official online application portal by the deadline of July 24, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST

For any additional questions not addressed in the FAQ, prospective applicants can reach out directly to the Goldman Sachs Community Development team at: gs-cd-grants@gs.com

You can find the 2026 Application Questions PDF here.

Read More and Apply Here

Why This Grant Matters for LMI Communities

General operating grants of the kind offered through both the Change Makers and Equity Builders initiatives are widely regarded as the most impactful and flexible form of philanthropic support that a nonprofit organization can receive. Unlike restricted project grants that can only be spent on specific activities, general operating grants give organizations the freedom to use the funding where it is most needed — whether that is hiring staff, upgrading technology, improving financial management, expanding programmes, or building reserves for resilience.

For nonprofits working in LMI communities in New York City, New Jersey, and Utah, where the cost of living is high and the funding landscape is competitive, a Goldman Sachs Community Development grant can be genuinely transformational. Beyond the financial support, selection as a Goldman Sachs Community Development Champion brings significant reputational recognition and the opportunity to be part of a network of high-impact organizations working toward a common goal of economic opportunity and mobility for all.

Conclusion

The Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant 2026 is a compelling and high-value funding opportunity for nonprofit organizations in New York, New Jersey, and Utah that are doing serious, measurable work to advance economic opportunity in low- and moderate-income communities. Whether through the one-time general operating grant of the Change Makers Initiative or the sustained, multi-year investment of the Equity Builders Initiative, Goldman Sachs is offering nonprofits the kind of flexible, unrestricted funding that can truly move the needle on community development.

With a track record of reaching over 808,000 community members and serving more than 2,000 small businesses through past grantees, the program has a proven record of supporting organizations that are making a real and lasting difference. The application deadline of July 24, 2026 at 11:59 PM EST is firm, and competition is expected to be strong. Eligible nonprofits should begin preparing their applications immediately, ensuring they can clearly demonstrate measurable impact, deep community engagement, and an innovative approach that goes beyond existing solutions. This is your opportunity to join a cohort of community development champions backed by one of the world’s most prominent financial institutions — do not miss it.

Discover more global Grant opportunities on OFY.

The post Goldman Sachs Community Development Champions Grant 2026: Everything Nonprofit Organizations in New York, New Jersey, and Utah Need to Know appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NEXUS 2026: German Marshall Fund Launches New Security Leadership Programme for MA and PhD Students in the US, Japan, and Europe
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The German Marshall Fund of the United States has launched a new leadership initiative called NEXUS (Next Generation Security Exchange), designed to prepare the next generation of security experts and policymakers working on nuclear issues and international cooperation. Funded by The Japan Foundation, NEXUS brings together emerging scholars from the United States, Japan, and Europe […]

The post NEXUS 2026: German Marshall Fund Launches New Security Leadership Programme for MA and PhD Students in the US, Japan, and Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The German Marshall Fund of the United States has launched a new leadership initiative called NEXUS (Next Generation Security Exchange), designed to prepare the next generation of security experts and policymakers working on nuclear issues and international cooperation.

Funded by The Japan Foundation, NEXUS brings together emerging scholars from the United States, Japan, and Europe for an intensive learning experience focused on nuclear security, strategic affairs, and international cooperation.

The programme is now accepting applications from outstanding Master’s and PhD students who want to deepen their understanding of nuclear policy while building professional networks across regions.

Application Deadline: 30 June 2026 at 11:59 p.m. ET

About the NEXUS Programme

NEXUS: Next Generation Security Exchange is a new leadership programme created to develop future leaders who can contribute to stronger:

  • US-Japan bilateral relations.
  • US-Japan-Europe trilateral cooperation.
  • Global nuclear security discussions.
  • International policy development.

The initiative focuses on preparing participants to become informed researchers, analysts, and policymakers who understand the complex security challenges shaping the world today.

The programme’s goal is to build a network of emerging experts who can contribute to future conversations on nuclear strategy, international security, and cooperation among key global partners.

Programme Objectives

NEXUS aims to:

  • Train future security professionals.
  • Strengthen cooperation between the United States, Japan, and Europe.
  • Develop deeper understanding of nuclear security challenges.
  • Encourage cross-regional collaboration among young researchers.
  • Support participants in becoming future policy leaders.

The programme recognizes the importance of developing a new generation of experts capable of addressing complex global security issues through research, dialogue, and cooperation.

Who Will Be Selected?

For its inaugural year, NEXUS will select:

10 Master’s and PhD students

from:

  • Japan.
  • Europe.
  • The United States.

Selected participants will form a diverse international cohort focused on exploring nuclear security issues and strengthening cooperation between regions.

Key Topics Covered During the Programme

Participants will explore major areas of nuclear policy and international security, including:

Nuclear Deterrence Strategy

Students will examine the role of nuclear deterrence in international relations and how strategic decisions influence global security.

Topics may include:

  • Security guarantees.
  • Military strategy.
  • Regional security dynamics.
  • Strategic stability.

International Nuclear History

Participants will study historical developments that have shaped today’s nuclear landscape.

This includes understanding:

  • Major nuclear events.
  • Evolution of nuclear policies.
  • Lessons from past international security challenges.

Nuclear Nonproliferation

The programme will explore efforts to prevent the spread of nuclear weapons and strengthen international security frameworks.

Participants will examine:

  • Nonproliferation agreements.
  • Global security mechanisms.
  • Challenges facing nuclear governance.

Alliance Politics

Students will analyze how partnerships between countries influence security decision-making.

Special focus will be placed on:

  • US-Japan relations.
  • US-Japan-Europe cooperation.
  • Regional security partnerships.

Programme Format

The NEXUS programme combines online learning with an in-person immersive experience.

The programme includes:

Interactive Webinars

Participants will take part in a series of virtual sessions designed to build knowledge, encourage discussion, and connect students with experts.

In-Person Training in Washington, DC

The virtual sessions will lead to an intensive in-person training programme in:

Washington, DC

Date:

8–13 November 2026

The in-person programme will provide opportunities for:

  • Direct engagement with experts.
  • Professional networking.
  • Policy discussions.
  • Practical learning experiences.

Participant Benefits

Selected participants will receive support to participate in the programme.

Benefits include:

  • Round-trip airfare to Washington, DC.
  • Accommodation.
  • Meals during the programme.
  • Access to expert-led training.
  • Opportunity to build international professional networks.
  • Exposure to security policy discussions.
  • Opportunity to publish a short policy-relevant article.

The programme provides participants with both academic knowledge and practical experience relevant to future careers in security and policymaking.

Publication Opportunity

Participants will have the opportunity to develop and publish a short policy-focused article.

This allows participants to:

  • Share their ideas with broader audiences.
  • Develop policy communication skills.
  • Strengthen their professional profiles.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

1. Resume

Requirements:

  • Maximum length: 2 pages.

The resume should highlight:

  • Academic background.
  • Research experience.
  • Relevant professional activities.
  • Security or policy interests.

2. Statement of Interest

Applicants must provide a short statement explaining:

  • Why they want to participate in NEXUS.
  • How they plan to apply knowledge gained from the programme.
  • How nuclear policy and trilateral cooperation connect with their future professional goals.

The statement should also confirm:

  • Ability to travel to Washington, DC from 8–13 November 2026.
  • Commitment to attending all virtual sessions.

For applicants based in Europe and Japan:

  • They should indicate whether they have previously visited Washington, DC.

Statement length:

Maximum 250 words

Application Timeline

Important dates:

  • Application deadline: 30 June 2026 at 11:59 p.m. ET
  • Virtual sessions: Before the in-person training
  • Washington, DC training: 8–13 November 2026

Applicants should prepare their materials carefully and submit before the deadline.

How to Apply

Interested students should apply through the German Marshall Fund career application platform.

Click here for more and to apply

Applicants should submit their resume and statement of interest through the application process.

References are not required. Applicants may enter “N/A” in reference sections where applicable.

Programme Experts

The programme is supported by experts from the German Marshall Fund’s Indo-Pacific team, including:

  • Sayuri Romei
  • Colleen Scribner
  • Jennifer Nguyen

Why This Opportunity Matters

NEXUS represents an important opportunity for emerging researchers who want to contribute to conversations about nuclear security and international cooperation.

By bringing together students from the United States, Japan, and Europe, the programme creates a platform for developing future leaders who can address global challenges through collaboration, research, and informed policy-making.

Conclusion

The NEXUS: Next Generation Security Exchange programme offers MA and PhD students a unique opportunity to expand their expertise in nuclear security, connect with international peers, and engage with leading experts in global affairs.

Students interested in security studies, international relations, and nuclear policy are encouraged to apply before the 30 June 2026 deadline.

Click here for more global fellowship opportunities

 

The post NEXUS 2026: German Marshall Fund Launches New Security Leadership Programme for MA and PhD Students in the US, Japan, and Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
